articles.description:
The McWrap has been reintroduced in the US at a single McDonald's location as part of the "Global Favorites" menu at the "global-inspired" restaurant inside McDonald's headquarters in Chicago.

The Global Favorites menu is a rotating menu that pulls McDonald's items from around the world. The current offerings include:
- McWrap Classic (Poland) - McCrispy Strips, shredded lettuce, American cheese, mayonnaise, Roma tomatoes, arugula, and Tomato Onion Sauce, wrapped up in a flour tortilla. It's priced at $6.99.
- McWrap Sweet Chili (France) - McCrispy Strips, shredded lettuce, American cheese, mayonnaise, Roma tomatoes, and Sweet Chili Sauce, wrapped up in a flour tortilla. It is also priced at $6.99. It's a bit different from the Sweet Chili McWrap offered here in the US over a decade ago.
- Big Arch Burger (Global but not in the US... yet) - Two quarter-pound beef patties, three slices of processed white cheddar cheese, crispy-fried onions, slivered onions, pickles, shredded lettuce, and Big Arch sauce on a toasted sesame and poppy seed bun. It's priced at $7.99.
- Biscoff McPops (Spain) - Mini donuts filled with Biscoff Cookie Butter and dusted with powdered sugar. It costs $3.99 for three. McPops also appeared on the menu of McDonald's CosMc's concept.
- Chocolate Hazelnut McPops (Spain) - Mini donuts with a chocolate hazelnut filling, dusted with powdered sugar. In Spain, they're just called Nutella McPops. They're priced at three for $3.99.
- Berry McPops (Switzerland) - Pink mini donuts filled with wild berry jam, dusted with pink powdered sugar. As with the others, it's priced at three for $3.99.
The restaurant is open to the public and located at 1035 W Randolph St, Chicago, IL 60607.
Photo via McDonald's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: McDonald's McWrap is Available at One Location in the US for a Limited Time

articles.description:
McAlister's Deli celebrates the 2025 Halloween season by bringing back its Witch's Brew and launching the new Pumpkin Pecan Cookie with White Chocolate Chips.

McAlister's Witch's Brew is a green-colored lemonade made with cane sugar, lemon juice, and caramel apple syrup.
The new Pumpkin Pecan Cookie with White Chocolate Chips features a soft, chewy pumpkin cookie made with warm spices (cinnamon, nutmeg, and ginger), pecans, and white chocolate chips.
Both menu items are available for a limited time now through October 31, 2025, at participating McAlister's Deli locations chain-wide, while supplies last.
Photo via McAlister's Deli.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: McAlister's Deli Welcomes Back Witch's Brew and Introduces New White Chocolate Chip Pumpkin Pecan Cookie

articles.description:
Jack in the Box launches the new Monster Munchies menu for the 2025 Halloween season.

Available now for a limited time, the snackable menu includes several oversized items and more. It includes:
- Two Monster Tacos - Two crispy tacos topped with melty American cheese, shredded lettuce, and taco sauce. They're slightly taller and a good deal longer than Jack in the Box's regular tacos. The suggested price starts at $3.
- Monster Mozzarella Stick  - A 6" breaded and fried mozzarella stick served with two marinara dip cups. Pricing starts at $3.
- Monster Churro - A 10" fried churro with a cinnamon sugar cream filling, dusted with cinnamon sugar. As with the tacos and mozzarella stick, pricing starts at $3.
- Halloween Monster Cookie Shake - A vanilla shake blended with Chips Ahoy! syrup and Oreo cookie crumbs, topped with whipped topping and more Oreo crumbs. Pricing starts at $4.99.
- Monster Cookie Sweet Cream Iced Coffee - For $3.99 (may vary), it's an iced coffee mixed with sweet cream and Chips Ahoy! syrup.
In addition to the seasonal menu items, Jack has also put together a Halloween-themed meal: the Monster Munchie Meal. It costs $12 (may vary) and includes:
- Two Monster Tacos
- A Monster Mozzarella Stick with two marinara dip cups
- Four chicken nuggets
- Seasoned curly fries
- A regular drink
- A collectible color-changing Halloween cup
Photo via Jack in the Box.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Jack in the Box Welcomes 2025 Halloween Menu

articles.description:
The Spicy Western Bacon Cheeseburger is back on the Carl's Jr. menu for a limited time. It takes the Western Bacon Cheeseburger and swaps in pepper jack cheese in place of American cheese and adds sliced jalapenos.

Specifically, the burger features a charbroiled beef patty, two strips of bacon, melted pepper jack cheese, crispy-fried onion rings, tangy BBQ sauce, and pickled jalapeno coins, served on a sesame seed bun. It's also available with two or three beef patties. 
It should be noted that both pepper jack cheese and pickled jalapeno coins can be found in some regular menu items at Carl's Jr., i.e., you can still custom-order the Spicy Western Bacon Cheeseburger after its limited-time run is over.
Photo via Carl's Jr.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Carl's Jr. Brings Back Spicy Western Bacon Cheeseburger

articles.description:
In time for football season, Dot's Homestyle Pretzels introduces a new Buffalo sauce pretzel flavor as a permanent addition to its product line.

The spicy and tangy flavor features "bold Buffalo heat" paired with the brand's "signature buttery crunch."
Dot's Homestyle Pretzels Buffalo Flavored Seasoned Twists are available now at retailers nationwide. You can find them in 5-oz, 9.5-oz, 16-oz, and 32-oz bags. Prices vary by retailers, but a 16-oz bag costs $6.99 on the Hershey's online store (Hershey's owns the Dot's Homestyle Pretzel brand).
Photo via Dot's Homestyle Pretzels.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: New Dot's Homestyle Pretzels Buffalo Flavored Seasoned Twists Arrive in Stores

articles.description:
Wendy's Boo! Books have made their annual return for Halloween, offering a booklet of five "free Jr. Frosty" coupons for $1.

The booklets also include a "bonus" coupon for a $1.99 Kids' Meal with any purchase. All six coupons can be redeemed through December 31, 2025.
90 cents from the purchase price of each book goes to benefit the Dave Thomas Foundation for Adoption.
You can buy the Boo! Books at any participating Wendy's restaurant or through the Wendy's mobile app or website (under the "Give Something Back" category). They can also be ordered in bulk through the Dave Thomas Foundation for Adoption website.
Photo via Wendy's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Boo! Books Return to Wendy's for Halloween 2025

articles.description:
Taco Bell is testing new Double Dipping Burritos for a limited time at participating locations in the Charlotte, NC area.

The burritos feature a choice of grilled marinated steak or slow-roasted chicken with a melty 3-cheese blend, and Avocado Ranch sauce, wrapped up in a flour tortilla and grilled.
You get two snack-sized burritos in an order, and they come with a split dipping cup. The cup is filled with Chili Lime Crema on one size and a crunchy blend of crushed seasoned tortilla chips and crispy cotija cheese.
Prices range between $5.99 and $6.99, depending on the location.
Taco Bell previously released something similar, Cheesy Dipping Burritos, earlier this year.
Photo via Taco Bell.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Taco Bell Tests New Double Dipping Burritos

articles.description:
Outback Steakhouse welcomes the fall with several featured dishes and a pair of color-changing platypus-themed drinks.

The seasonal menu items include:
- Down Under Trio - A shareable platter Bloomin' Onion petals with signature sauce, new Fried Mozzarella Bloomerangs with marinara, and Aussie Cheese Fries with house-made ranch.
- Three Cheese Spinach Dip (limited time only) - Made with Mozzarella, Parmesan, cream cheese, and Alfredo sauce. It comes with tortilla chips for dipping.
- Filet with Roasted Garlic Butter - A barrel-cut filet topped with roasted garlic butter and paired with a choice of steakhouse potato and a side.
- Platypus Punch (pictured; limited time only)- Deep Eddy Grapefruit Vodka, mango, and Country Style Lemonade, served with a color-changing tea egg and a keepsake platypus.
- Puggle Splash (limited time only)- A non-alcoholic version of Platypus Punch made with mango lemonade and blue tea.
- Kangaroo Court (limited time only)- A Shiraz Cabernet Sauvignon blend created by winemakers Mollydooker.
Photo via Outback Steakhouse.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Outback Serves Up New Platypus-Themed Drinks and More

articles.description:
Hungry Howie's honors its Detroit roots (the pizza chain was founded in the Detroit suburbs in 1973) with the launch of its new Detroit-Style Pizza.

The new pizza features a rectangular pizza with a "thick, airy crust, crispy caramelized edges, cheese layered to the edge, and sauce added as the final topping."
Hungry Howie's Detroit-Style Pizza is available now at participating locations chain-wide. Each pizza includes eight slices, and prices start at $12.99 (it's $14.99 here in Los Angeles).
Photo via Hungry Howie's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Hungry Howie's Introduces New Detroit-Style Pizza

articles.description:
The white chocolate version of a Crunch bar, Crunch White, is back in stores for a limited time after a 20-year hiatus.

Where the Crunch bar features a milk chocolate candy bar with crisped rice, the Crunch White replaces the milk chocolate with white chocolate.
The limited-time candy bar arrives in time for the 2025 Halloween season and can be found in 1.55-oz single-size bars as well as 2.7-oz Share packs. Crunch White bars are available now through December 2025 at retailers nationwide.
Photo via Ferrero.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: White Chocolate Crunch Bars are Back

articles.description:
Pizza Hut's Chicken Bacon Ranch Crafted Flatzz is a "hand-crafted" personal pizza topped with crispy-fried boneless chicken pieces, applewood-smoked bacon, Ultimate Ranch sauce, mozzarella, and a parmesan oregano seasoning.

I got one for $5 during lunch time (the price goes up to $6.99 after 5PM). I also reviewed the Nashville Hot Chicken one earlier.



The Crafted Flatzz is about twice the size of a Pizza Hut personal pan pizza, but a good deal thinner (and less greasy) with a hand-stretched oval crust. It was somewhat football-shaped, which is fitting for the season, I suppose. It was very crispy on the surface while maintaining a soft crumb inside.
The pizza was cut into eight two-bite (or one big bite) slices with one small popcorn chicken-esque crispy chicken piece per slice.



The mozzarella was nicely melted, but the underlying Ultimate Ranch just tasted like your run-of-the-mill ranch to me. Between the two, there was a moderate creaminess and a herby tang throughout the pizza. The parmesan oregano didn't make much of an impact.
The bacon was a little sparse here, and I could have used a little more to cut against the strong ranch flavor. I feel like it's one of those "your mileage may vary" things, though.

Overall, Pizza Hut's Chicken Bacon Ranch Crafted Flatzz is nice for the price and crust, but leans too heavily on ranch as its primary flavor driver. Typically, when something is called "chicken bacon ranch," it's mostly chicken, then bacon, then ranch. This was more like "ranch chicken bacon." I would definitely choose the Nashville Hot Chicken Crafted Flatzz over it.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Review: Pizza Hut - Chicken Bacon Ranch Crafted Flatzz

articles.description:
Cold Stone adopts a carnival theme this fall by launching new Churro ice cream and reintroducing Honey Cornbread with Blackberry Jam ice cream.


As is typical for the ice cream parlor chain, the two fall flavors are featured in two Creations, including:
- New Caramel Carnival Churro Creation (pictured) - Churro ice cream folded with Cinnamon Toast Crunch, caramel, and sugar crystals.
- Cornbread is My Jam Creation - Honey Cornbread with Blackberry Jam Ice Cream mixed with cornbread, honey, and blueberries.
Both 2025 fall ice cream flavors and their associated Creations are available now at all Cold Stone Creamery locations for a limited time through November 18, 2025.
Photo via Cold Stone Creamery.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Cold Stone Welcomes Carnival -Themed Flavors for Fall 2025

articles.description:
Papa Johns introduces the new Papa Dippa, which the pizza chain is calling the "first-ever pizza designed exclusively for dippin'. It's joined by three new dipping sauces: Roasted Parmesan, Garlic Hot Honey, and Creamy Garlic Pesto.

The Papa Dippa is a large, three-cheese (provolone, Fontina, and Asiago) pepperoni pizza with a flattened edge cut into 16 strips/sticks instead of slices. It comes with a "Garlic Sauce Flight," which includes the three new sauces as well as the chain's signature Special Garlic Sauce.
Here's how Papa Johns describes the new dipping sauces:


Roasted Garlic Parmesan - "A Caesar-style blend with roasted garlic and Parmesan cheese."
Garlic Hot Honey - "Sweet heat with garlic and chili flakes for an added kick.​"
Creamy Garlic Pesto - "Smooth yet textured with basil and Parmesan for a rich, herbaceous dip."


The Papa Dippa is available now for a limited time nationwide for $13.99 (may vary; it's $14.99 at my local Papa Johns here in Los Angeles).
Nutritional Info - Papa Johns Papa Dippa
Serving Size - 1 stick
Calories - 160
Fat - 6g (Saturated Fat - 3g)
Sodium - 340mg
Carbs - 18g (Sugar - 2g)
Protein - 6g
Photo via Papa Johns.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Papa Johns Debuts New Papa Dippa

articles.description:
Taco Bell updates its Freeze menu with the addition of the new Orange Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze, as well as a non-orange-flavored version, the Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze.

The Orange Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze features a sweet vanilla frozen, carbonated slushy base swirled with sweet vanilla creamer and tangy orange syrup. Pricing starts at $3.69 for a regular, $3.89 for a large. Regular Freezes of any flavor are also discounted to $1 during the chain's daily Happier Hour from 2 to 5PM.
The Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze is the same as the Orange Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze, minus the orange-flavored syrup. Pricing is the same as well.
Photo via Taco Bell.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Taco Bell Launches New Orange Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze

articles.description:
Popeyes partners with popular YouTube series Hot Ones to debut the new Popeyes x Hot Ones menu, featuring Hot Ones-inspired dishes with varying degrees of heat.

Available for a limited time at participating locations nationwide, the Popeyes x Hot Ones menu includes:


Sizzlin' Sriracha Dippers (Mild) - Pickled garlic sriracha-marinated Chicken Dippers, served with a Buttermilk Ranch dip cup and a Hot Ones Sriracha sachet.
Smokin' Rojo Sandwich (Medium)- A crispy-fried chicken filet topped with Hot Ones' Los Calientes Rojo spread and pickles, on a toasted brioche bun.
Darin' Dab Ghost Wings (Hot) - Your choice of bone-in or boneless wings dusted with a Ghost Pepper dry rub and served with a Hot Ones Last Dab Ranch dip cup.
The Last Dab (Extremely Hot) - A sachet of Hot Ones' spiciest, signature hot sauce. It's available a la carte for $1.


For those who want to do their own Hot Ones-type challenge, Popeyes has put together the Popeyes x Hot Ones bundle. It includes the Sizzlin' Sriracha Dippers, a Sriracha sachet, a Smokin' Rojo Sandwich, and Darin' Dab Ghost Wings (bone-in or boneless) with Last Dab Ranch for $16.99.
As part of the collaboration, the Popeyes x Hot Ones menu will be featured on an episode of the show, making Popeyes the first wing brand to be spotlighted on the show.
Photo via Popeyes.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: New Popeyes x Hot Ones Menu Arrives

articles.description:
Taco Bell looks into expanding its Energy Refresca lineup with a test of the new Electric Punch Energy Refresca and Berry Burst Energy Refresca for a limited time at participating locations in the San Francisco Bay area.

The Electric Punch Energy Refresca features a tropical punch-flavored, fizzy drink with green coffee extract, L-theanine, vitamin B12, and guarana for energy. It contains 160mg of caffeine.
The Berry Burst Energy Refresca is a berry-flavored, carbonated drink infused with green coffee extract, L-theanine, vitamin B12, and guarana. It contains 160mg of caffeine.
Both drinks are available for a suggested price of $4.99. Taco Bell currently serves the Tropical Punch Rockstar Energy Refresca and the Pineapple Lime Rockstar Energy Refresca nationwide for the same suggested price.
Photo via Taco Bell.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Taco Bell Testing Two New Energy Refresca Flavors

articles.description:
Taco John's serves up new Jr. Crispy Chicken Wraps for $1.79 each. Each wrap features a familiar combination of a crispy-fried white-meat chicken tender, shredded lettuce, a shredded four-cheese blend, and ranch, rolled up in a warm flour tortilla.

Regular and spicy versions are available, with the only difference between the two being the addition of pickled jalapenos in the spicy variety.
For a limited time, Jr. Crispy Chicken Wraps are also available as part of a two-for-$3 mix-and-match deal. You can also get them as part of the $5 Jr. Crispy Chicken Wrap Meal, which includes two of the wraps and Jr. Potato Olés.
In addition to the new snackable wraps, Taco John's has made several of its Meal Steals available for in-person ordering (the value combos were previously only available when ordering online). The following Meal Steals are now available to order in person as well as digitally:


$5 Meal Steal - A Crispy Beef Taco, a Bean Burrito, Jr. Potato Olés, and a small fountain drink.
$7 Meal Steal - Two Crispy Beef Tacos, a Nacho Crunch Beef Burrito, Jr. Potato Olés, and a small fountain drink.
$9 Meal Steal - A Super Crispy Beef Taco, a Chicken Stuffed Grilled Taco, Jr. Potato Olés, a Churro, and a small fountain drink.


The $7 Burrito Bundle and $7 Taco Takeover remain as online-only specials.
Photo via Taco John's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Taco John's Introduces New Jr. Crispy Chicken Wraps

articles.description:
The all-you-can-eat Never Ending Pasta Bowl promotion is back at Olive Garden for a limited time through Sunday, November 16, 2025.

Starting at $13.99, you can choose from four pastas, six sauces, and three all-you-can-eat toppings (for an additional $4.99. The Never Ending Pasta Bowl selection includes:


Pastas - Angel Hair, Fettuccine, Rigatoni, and Spaghetti
Sauces - Alfredo, Five Cheese Marinara, Meat Sauce, and Spicy Three-Meat Sauce.
Toppings - Crispy Chicken Fritta, Meatballs, and Italian Sausage


The Spicy Three-Meat Sauce is new to the promotion. It's also fairly new to Olive Garden, as it was introduced earlier this year. It's a meat sauce flavored with cherry peppers, chili flakes, garlic, herbs, pepperoni, red bell pepper, and red wine.
Your meal also includes unlimited soup or salad, plus unlimited breadsticks.
Tips for making the most of the promotion include asking for smaller portions to sample the various offerings and asking them to prep your next bowl as you're working on your current one, as there can be a bit of a wait otherwise.
Photo via Olive Garden.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Never Ending Pasta Bowl is Back at Olive Garden

articles.description:
Taco Bell is testing the new Cantina Chicken Double Tostada for a limited time at participating locations in the Austin, TX area.

Resembling Taco Bell's Mexican Pizza, the new test item features a layer of slow-roasted chicken and black beans sandwiched by two crunchy tostada shells, topped with Avocado Verde sauce, a melted three-cheese blend (pepper jack, mozzarella, and cheddar), reduced-fat sour cream, purple cabbage, and pico de gallo. As with other Cantina Chicken menu items, a packet of Avocado Verde Salsa is included on the side.
The Cantina Chicken Double Tostada is priced at $6.99. It's also available in a combo that includes a Cantina Chicken Double Tostada, a Soft Taco, and an Aqua Refresca for $10.49.
Photo via Taco Bell.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Taco Bell Testing New Cantina Chicken Double Tostada

articles.description:
Subway brings back its better-for-you Fresh Fit menu nationwide on September 17, 2025 (some locations are already offering the menu).

Each sandwich on the Fresh Fit menu features 20g or more protein, a full serving of veggies, and less than 500 calories in a six-inch sub. They include:
- Grilled Chicken & Avocado (35g protein) - Grilled chicken, smashed avocado, lettuce, spinach, tomatoes, cucumbers, red onions, and smoky Baja Chipotle sauce on multigrain bread.
- Ham & Turkey Stacker (20g protein) - Oven-roasted turkey and Black Forest ham, lettuce, spinach, tomatoes, cucumbers, red onions, and yellow mustard on multigrain bread.
- Seasoned Steak & Avocado (35g protein) - Seasoned steak, smashed avocado, lettuce, spinach, tomatoes, and red onions on multigrain bread.
- Turkey & Ranch Delite (26g protein) - Oven-roasted turkey, lettuce, spinach, tomatoes, red onions, and Peppercorn Ranch on multigrain bread.
According to Subway, the new menu "returns with more flavor and options to fuel today's active lifestyles."
Photo via Subway.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Fresh Fit Menu Returns to Subway

articles.description:
Krispy Kreme turns to fairs for inspiration in launching its new Fall Fair Collection of seasonal donuts.

Available for a limited time at participating locations chain-wide, the collection includes four donuts:
- Cotton Candy Donut - An Original Glazed donut dipped in blue icing and cotton candy-flavored sugar, then topped with a strawberry-flavored icing swirl and a dollop of cotton candy-flavored buttercreme. 
- Kreme Puff Donut - A ring donut topped with a swirl of Kreme, a Donut Dot (donut hole), and a dusting of sweet powdered coating.
- Blue Ribbon Apple Pie Donut - A shell donut with apple cinnamon filling, dipped in caramel-flavored icing, sprinkled with cinnamon cookie crumbs, and decorated with a white icing cross-hatch.
- Caramel Churro Donut - A ring donut covered in cinnamon sugar and drizzled with caramel-flavored icing.
Krispy Kreme's Fall Fair Collection can be purchased individually and by the dozen.
Photo via Krispy Kreme.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Krispy Kreme Launches New Fall Fair Donuts

articles.description:
Ciabatta Dippers are back on the menu at Panera for a limited time nationwide, and are available in two varieties: the returning French Dipper and the new Chicken Pesto Dipper.

The melty sandwiches come with a portion of soup for dipping.
The French Dipper features marinated steak, provolone, asiago, and garlic aioli on ciabatta, served with Bistro French Onion Soup.
The new Chicken Pesto Dipper slots in for the previous Spicy Chicken Dipper and includes grilled chicken, provolone, parmesan, and basil pesto on ciabatta, paired with Creamy Tomato Soup.
Prices vary, but the French Dipper costs $13.69 near me, while the Chicken Pesto Dipper is $11.99.

articles.description:
Wendy's is in the process of quietly switching from whole-leafed romaine lettuce to shredded iceberg lettuce for its burgers and chicken sandwiches. The switch began this summer at some locations and recently appeared here in Los Angeles.

The move was reportedly made to improve lettuce consistency and maintain a reliable supply. Where the romaine lettuce was hand-leafed at each location, the shredded iceberg lettuce arrives already prepared in bags, so there's a convenience factor as well.
The lettuce change does not affect the chain's salads, which, depending on the salad, are made with romaine and/or spring mix.
Personal preferences between shredded lettuce and leafed lettuce on a burger/sandwich can vary. Generally, leaf lettuce tends to wilt faster as there's typically more contact between the burger patty and the lettuce, while shredded lettuce occupies more volume, which provides more of a buffer against heat. However, shredded lettuce is also a lot messier as it falls out of your burger/sandwich easily. It's also cheaper.
Additionally, for anyone who was getting lettuce-wrapped burgers, that's no longer an option at locations that have made the switch.
Wendy's has not yet updated its website to reflect the change as of this writing (pictured). I recently got a burger from my local Wendy's and definitely noticed the shredded lettuce, but didn't note any mention of it during my visit.
Photo via Wendy's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Wendy's Switching to Shredded Lettuce in Burgers

articles.description:
Chicken Finger Tacos are back at Zaxbys and have brought along a new flavor: Buffalo Blue.

The new Buffalo Blue Taco features a hand-breaded chicken finger, pico de gallo, blue cheese crumbles, shredded iceberg lettuce, and a drizzle of Buffalo Garlic Blaze sauce and Ranch, served folded in a warm flour tortilla.
The new taco is joined by the returning Chipotle Ranch with Pico Taco and Avocado Ranch BLT Taco.
The Chipotle Ranch with Pico Taco includes a hand-breaded chicken finger, shredded iceberg lettuce, cheddar jack cheese, Pico de Gallo, and mildly spicy Chipotle Ranch sauce, in a warm flour tortilla.
The Avocado Ranch BLT Taco features a hand-breaded chicken finger, sliced Roma tomatoes, hardwood-smoked bacon, shredded iceberg lettuce, and a drizzle of Avocado Ranch, served folded in a warmed flour tortilla.
Chicken Finger Tacos are available for a limited time now through November 2, 2025. They're available a la carte or as part of a combo meal. Prices vary by location, but at a location in Atlanta, a single taco costs $3.99, while three tacos and a drink cost $11.29.
Photo via Zaxbys.
